message:
style: replace needless raw string hashes with plain raw strings (clippy)

Fix clippy::needless_raw_string_hashes warnings in proptest strategies:

- src/providers/gemini.rs: r#"{}"# → r"{}"
- src/session.rs: 6 instances of r#"..."# → r"..." where no quotes
  or special characters require the hash delimiters (e.g., r#"{}"#,
  r#"[1,2,3]"#, r#"42"#, r#"null"#, r#"true"#, r#"not json at all"#)

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl with a review comment on bd-b4s4l
(session picker UX refinement note).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-b1d7o","title":"[SEC-4.1] Per-extension resource quotas (CPU, memory, hostcalls, subprocess budget)","description":"## Background
Resource abuse is both a DoS vector and a cover for malicious behavior.

## Scope
- Add configurable quotas per extension for CPU time, memory growth, hostcall rates, and subprocess fan-out.
- Trigger deterministic enforcement actions on quota breaches.
- Ensure quotas are profile-aware and visible to operators.

## Deliverables
- Quota engine and policy config schema.
- Quota breach telemetry and tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Quota violations are detected and enforced consistently.
- [ ] Quota counters reset semantics are explicit and tested.
- [ ] Benign workloads remain within default quotas.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:41.190571760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T08:23:24.063161047Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T08:23:24.063063609Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["containment","quotas","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b1d7o","depends_on_id":"bd-2ezm9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:59.082265717Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1373,"issue_id":"bd-b1d7o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RainyMill (claude-opus-4-6) claiming bd-b1d7o. Plan: implement per-extension resource quota engine in src/extensions.rs with configurable limits for hostcall rates, memory growth tracking, and subprocess budget. Will add quota breach telemetry, enforcement actions, and profile-aware configuration. Will coordinate via Agent Mail to avoid conflicts with adjacent SEC beads.","created_at":"2026-02-14T07:45:36Z"},{"id":1377,"issue_id":"bd-b1d7o","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RainyMill: SEC-4.1 implementation complete.  {"id":"bd-b36e3","title":"[PERF-3] Frame timing telemetry: instrument view()/update() with μs-precision timing","description":"## Problem

TUI framerate target is 60fps but there is ZERO measurement infrastructure. Cannot optimize what we cannot measure.

## Solution: Frame Timing Instrumentation

Add microsecond-precision timing to the TUI rendering pipeline, gated behind PI_PERF_TELEMETRY=1 environment variable.

### CRITICAL DESIGN NOTE: view() is &self

The charmed_bubbletea Model trait defines `fn view(&self) -> String`. This means we CANNOT mutate FrameTimingStats inside view(). Two approaches:

**Approach A (Recommended): Interior mutability with Cell/RefCell**
```rust
struct FrameTimingStats {
    /// Rolling window of last 60 frame render times
    frame_times_us: RefCell<VecDeque<u64>>,
    /// Rolling window of last 60 content build times
    content_build_times_us: RefCell<VecDeque<u64>>,
    /// Rolling window of last 60 viewport sync times
    viewport_sync_times_us: RefCell<VecDeque<u64>>,
    /// Total frames rendered
    total_frames: Cell<u64>,
    /// Frames exceeding 16ms budget
    budget_exceeded_count: Cell<u64>,
    /// Whether telemetry is active
    enabled: bool,
}
```
- Use `RefCell<VecDeque<u64>>` for rolling windows (need borrow_mut in view())
- Use `Cell<u64>` for simple counters
- This is safe because TUI is single-threaded (bubbletea event loop)
- Cost: RefCell borrow check is ~1ns, negligible vs microsecond-level measurements

**Approach B: Measure in view(), report via Cmd**
- In view(), capture start/end Instant, return result String
- Emit a Cmd::perform() that sends a PiMsg::FrameTimingRecord back to update()
- update() stores the timing in &mut self
- Downside: one-frame delay in recording; more complex plumbing
- Upside: no RefCell needed

**Decision: Use Approach A** — simpler, no plumbing, single-threaded safety is obvious, and the Cell/RefCell pattern is idiomatic for interior mutability in Rust.

### Instrumentation Points
1. **view()** — Total frame render time (from entry to String return). Uses RefCell for recording.
2. **build_conversation_content()** — Content assembly time
3. **refresh_conversation_viewport()** — Viewport sync time (set_content + goto_bottom)
4. **update()** — Event handling time (not rendering, but useful for budget). Can use &mut self directly.

### Output
- Every 60 frames (1 second at 60fps), emit to tracing::debug:
  ```
  [perf] p50=2.1ms p95=4.3ms p99=12.1ms budget_exceeded=1/60 cache_hit=98%
  ```
- Also expose via /session command (add perf stats section when telemetry enabled)

### Zero-Cost When Disabled
- Check `self.frame_timing.enabled` at each instrumentation point
- When false: no Instant::now() calls, no VecDeque operations
- Enabled via: `PI_PERF_TELEMETRY=1` env var, checked once at app startup

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: Add FrameTimingStats field, instrument view()/update()/build_conversation_content()

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FrameTimingStats struct with RefCell<VecDeque> for rolling windows
- [ ] Cell<u64> for simple counters
- [ ] p50/p95/p99 calculation (on sorted copy of VecDeque)
- [ ] tracing::debug output every 60 frames when enabled
- [ ] Zero overhead when disabled (no Instant::now() calls)
- [ ] Telemetry visible in /session output when enabled
- [ ] Unit test: FrameTimingStats accumulation and percentile math (can test directly, no TUI needed)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (telemetry disabled by default)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:13:43.110291213Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:57:11.287880468Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:57:11.287807823Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria verified: FrameTimingStats struct with RefCell/Cell, p50/p95/p99 percentiles, tracing::debug emission every 60 frames, zero-cost when disabled, /session integration, and 12 passing unit tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-bc2z","title":"Validate unmodified provenance","description":"# Goal
Prove that vendored artifacts are byte‑for‑byte upstream and unmodified.

# Deliverables
- Verification checklist for each artifact (hash match, no local edits).
- Any exceptions documented with explicit rationale (should be none).
- Evidence log for auditability.

# Notes
This is a hard requirement: extensions must work without modification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:52.321135767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:04:37.247900503Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:04:37.247808491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bc2z","depends_on_id":"bd-2oal","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:26:52.337750673Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-bc2z","depends_on_id":"bd-3uvd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:28:19.245463193Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":342,"issue_id":"bd-bc2z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: The project mandate is to prove extensions work without modification.

Reasoning: Integrity validation is the only way to assert that artifacts are upstream‑exact.

Considerations: Keep evidence logs so future audits can verify the claim.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:51:24Z"},{"id":664,"issue_id":"bd-bc2z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: Provenance verification with evidence log.

Deliverables:
1. **Verification checklist**: tests/ext_provenance_verification.rs - comprehensive test that checks ALL 208 artifacts with 7 verification checks per artifact (directory exists, ID naming, layout, on-disk checksum, master catalog checksum, provenance checksum, consistency, source URL, license).

2. **Evidence log**: tests/ext_conformance/artifacts/PROVENANCE_VERIFICATION.json (schema: pi.ext.provenance_verification.v1) - structured JSON with per-artifact verification results + summary. Generated fresh each test run.

3. **Exceptions**: NONE - all 208 artifacts verified with 100% pass rate. Every artifact's on-disk SHA-256 matches both the master catalog and provenance manifest. All have source URLs and licenses.

4. **Cross-validation**: Three-way checksum comparison (on-disk vs master catalog vs provenance) ensures no local edits exist.

Supporting infrastructure from bd-1pqf:
- pi::conformance::snapshot module with digest_artifact_dir(), validate_id(), validate_directory(), SourceTier enum","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:04:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-bow2","title":"Conformance: Tier 5 - Multi-file official extensions (9 exts)","description":"# Conformance: Tier 5 - Multi-file official extensions (9 exts)

## Extensions
doom-overlay, plan-mode, subagent, sandbox, with-deps, custom-provider-anthropic, custom-provider-gitlab-duo, custom-provider-qwen-cli, dynamic-resources

## What These Test
These extensions have:
- Multiple source files (index.ts imports from sibling .ts files)
- npm dependencies (package.json with external packages)
- Complex initialization logic
- Provider registration (custom model providers)

## Why Multi-File Is Harder
- swc must handle import/require across files
- QuickJS module resolution must match Bun/jiti resolution
- npm deps need to be available (node_modules or bundled)
- Relative imports (./utils, ./agents/planner) must resolve correctly

## Provider Extensions (3)
custom-provider-anthropic, custom-provider-gitlab-duo, custom-provider-qwen-cli
These register custom model providers via registerProvider(). They require:
- OAuth mock (some use OAuthLoginCallbacks)
- HTTP mock (API calls to provider endpoints)
- Provider config matching between runtimes

## Sandbox/With-Deps Extensions
- sandbox/: has package.json with Docker integration deps
- with-deps/: has package.json with npm deps that must be installed

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 9 extensions attempted
- 7/9 pass (allowing failure for extensions requiring Docker or specific npm packages)
- Provider registration output matches between runtimes
- Import resolution verified for multi-file extensions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:23:14.406153849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:55:02.470270390Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:55:02.470119799Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bow2","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:23:14.415160662Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-bow2","depends_on_id":"bd-21dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:23:14.423573307Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
